fact: September 25 sits exactly 268 days into the year, which means there are only 97 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is prime time for squirrels to go absolutely nuts collecting acorns - they can bury up to 10,000 nuts in a single season!
The sun rises almost exactly due east and sets due west around this date because we're so close to the autumn equinox.
Monarch butterflies are typically in the middle of their epic 3,000-mile journey south right now, and some will live 8 times longer than their summer cousins just to make this trip!
September 25 falls smack in the middle of Libra season, making it one of the most 'balanced' days of the year according to astrology fans.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, spring is exploding right now and wombats are coming out of their winter hideouts looking extra chunky.
Maple trees in northern regions are starting to shut down their green chlorophyll factories, revealing the secret red and orange colors that were hiding in their leaves all summer long!
The moon often looks extra bright around this time of year because autumn air is clearer and less humid than summer air.
September 25 is exactly 13 weeks away from Christmas Day - that's a perfect quarter of a year!
Spiders seem to be everywhere right now because this is their mating season, and male spiders are desperately searching for girlfriends before winter hits.
In northern climates, the first frost can happen any day now, turning spider webs into glittery crystal masterpieces by morning.
Geese flying south typically create their famous V-formation around this time, and the bird at the front works 20% harder than the ones behind it!
